When you start to click phones, our enterprise members get 70 funnels, 20,000 contacts, 100,000 monthly visits, and a whole bunch more. That means that you're gonna be able to create opt-in funnels so you can generate leads, you can build sales funnels to sell your products, you can do webinar funnels, you can do automated webinar funnels, product launch funnels, membership funnels, and a whole bunch more. But not only are you able to do all that cool crap, you're also gonna be able to get rid of a whole bunch of stuff. Yes, that means that you can actually, in fact, our average click funnel members stays over $1,000 a month, the minute they start using click phones, because all this stuff, they can get rid of. So what that means is if you're using any other landing page software on Planet Earth, I don't care if it's lead pages, unbounce, whatever, if you're using them, you can cancel your accounts. Our number one competitor now charges $97 a month and all they do is landing pages, and you can't even move things on the landing pages. So you can cancel all of your landing page software, you can get rid of all those things, you can cancel any split testing software, you can get rid of your shopping cart, or if you have a shopping cart you love, bring it to us, we probably already integrate with it. You can get rid of your email audit response, your text message audit response, your automated webinar software, your affiliate software, you can get rid of your member site software, Photoshop, WordPress, cancel your website hosting, fire your programmers, fire your designers, fire your web ambassadors, and a whole bunch more. I want this to be free for them. Free, free, free. So when I come back through here, when you create your account, you can be able to do all this cool stuff, that's what I want anywhere. I want to be able to create all these funnels like Russell talked about, but the cool thing is it's gonna be free, because you can get rid of, this is all stuff you can get rid of, all stuff that goes away when you invest. It's just like in a regular funnel, every people are always asking, what should it be the price of my first upsell and my second upsell, I'm like, that's the wrong question. Number one, a lot of times you have testimonials, right? And what a lot of people do is first off, they capture testimonials at an event room standing in front of a white wall like this, the horrible shadow, and they're like, hi, my name is Julie, I went to Russell's seminar and it was awesome. If you have a chance to go, you should go someday. It'd be cool, right? And there's like 50 of those videos like that. They're horrible. And their slides are like, I got to test someone from Julie, hold on. And it's like, hi, my name is Julie. And it's like, it kills all momentum. When I'm going to this stack and close, this is like a two hour presentation in 30 minutes. You'll notice that my speed picks up. I go more rapid, I talk faster. It's all by design, right? And so when I'm coming here, if I have a video, it's going to slow everything down. I also didn't say, hey, Liz, Benny, can you tell me a testimonial? Because guess what happens when people just give you a testimony of their own free will and choice. They give you stupid things. The right to like a novel about something is horrible. So I just say, hey, Liz, what are your results? And she's like, this is awesome. I'm like, oh, that's so cool. Can I share with my audience? She's like, sure, I'm like, is this paragraph true? And I write it out. And she's like, yeah, sure, am I cool? Can I share this as your testimonial? She's like, yep. Cool. So I can architect what I needed to say based on the truth, but I don't leave it to them, because they will screw it up every single time. Okay? Very, very important. And then instead of like having a video of her playing it, I read it. Okay, I have a couple reasons. First off, I can give emphasis on the things that I need that are very, very important. Okay? And so I'm able to go through and tell the whole thing. And then after, what happens a lot of times is we share a success story or testimony or whatever. Mostly like, here's what Julie said. Okay, and they move right on. Okay, do you talk about trial closes at all? I'm not sure, a little bit. Just part of the little bit. I'm gonna talk about trial closes for a minute. But what trial closes are, after you share the most impactful things, instead of moving on, you wanna stop and you're gonna drop like 10 or 15 or 20 trial closes to cement that into someone's mind. Okay? So I'm gonna recap what a trial closes is for those who don't know. I learned initially, there's a guy named Ted Thomas. Have you guys ever seen Ted Thomas speak? If you Google Ted Thomas on YouTube and Pied Piper, his nickname is The Pied Piper. And there's videos of him at like Robert Kiyosaki events with like two, three, 4,000 people in the room. And he's in the front of the room. He just does his presentation. And he's got a line of like, I don't know, 700, 800 people behind him with their credit cards in hand all walking like this. And he's walking them all to the back of the room. Okay, they call him The Pied Piper. And I've heard legends of this guy for years. And I was like, I've never met him, never seen him outside of the videos on YouTube. I was like, I wanna meet this guy someday. So I'm in an event in Florida, on stage doing my thing, doing my best. And all of a sudden I see this legend walking the door and it's Ted Thomas. And I was like, oh my gosh, that's The Pied Piper. I'm about to transition to my clothes. What am I gonna do? And he sits down and I was like, oh, this is so awkward. So I'm doing my whole thing and I'm trying to like be the best I can be. Cause I'm like, The Pied Piper is in the room watching me close to like this audience. So I did my clothes and it was pretty good. Like, you know, it wasn't bad, but in comparison, I was kind of embarrassed, but like it was good, right? So he did the clothes and he's kind of hanging out afterwards. And then after he's done, he's like, hey, can I take you to lunch? I was like, yeah, that'd be awesome. So he takes me to lunch and he starts asking me some questions about some stuff and I'm listening to him, I'm talking and then he says, hey, Russell, notice what's happening in your head right now? I'm like, no, what? He's like, look at it. I'm like, what? He's like, you're nodding your head. And I'm like, oh yeah. He said, you know what I'm doing to you right now? I'm like, I have no idea. Like you totally just like, I'm totally lost. And he was like, I'm doing this thing called trial closes. He's like, when I sat in your room, he's like, the audience was engaged with you. But if you look from the back forward, everyone's heads were just sitting there. He's like, if you walk in the room and I'm giving you a presentation, it's like a wave of heads the entire time, the entire presentation from the very first word I start to the very end, everyone's going like this. It's just, he's like, guess how I get people to say yes. I'm like, I have no idea. He's like, I ask him questions. Well, the answer is always yes. And then they have to say yes. And that starts from origin story. And it goes through here and go through here and go through here. Okay. And he said, what do you need to do? You write down, you call them trial codes. Little questions, little things you ask people to give them to say yes. He said, next time you get to an event, he's like, write them out on little note cards and put them on the ground. And every time you walk and you touch one, look down and then ask the question. And then walk around, step down and ask the question. Now, I never dared do that. But when I'm back home from my next webinar, I went and made sticky notes. And I had a bunch of trial codes that put on sticky notes. And I was doing my presentation, I would like, look at it and say trial codes, and I say another trial codes, and I say another trial codes. At the time that I learned this from him, we had an automated webinar that had been running for probably a year or so. That hadn't touched the presentation since then. And I was like, I wonder if this trial closing is actually legit or if he's just kind of crazy. So I rewatched the entire webinar and I paused every time I thought I could put a trial close in. And I wrote out what it was and I recorded myself on a microphone saying the trial codes. Like in four or five tonalities. And I sent it to my brother who's a video editor. I said, okay, here take the whole video and plug in these trial codes that this minute, this minute, this minute, this minute, this minute, the whole thing, right? So we plugged it all in, we reuploaded the webinar and everything stayed the same. And our profit per register went from $9.60 per register to like $16.60 per register. Every trial code, if you've read the Docum Secrets Labs 108 split test book, I have the timeline of what I put the trial closes and what every single one was in that actual book and then the dollar amounts before and after. All was the trial closes. So trial closes just me asking a question. If you ever heard me say, are you guys getting this? What do you have to say? Yes. Okay. Is that awesome? Can you imagine being Liz Benny? Wouldn't that be cool? Like, I don't know about you guys, but if I made $100,000 in a month, like that would change my life. That change your guys' life right now? What would your spouse or your family think about that? If you came home and that's what happened after you did this, it'd be pretty amazing, right? Can I just get this? Okay. So what I do is after I share this testimony, I'm gonna ask 10 or 15 or 20 questions and they're gonna be like, wow, yes, yes, yes, yes. Okay, same thing happening throughout my origin story. When I tell these stories, I'm telling stuff, I'm asking like, I don't know about you, like would you guys do that? Does that make you nervous? Like is that awesome? Or is that like, I'm just always asking these things. And eventually as you start doing this, it'll become part of your language pattern. You'll start doing trial closes, everybody know all the time. Your spouse will be nodding her head, like your kids, like everyone. And for me, it's like a language pattern. Like if you watch my podcast, my videos, my everything, like everything we've done the last two days, you watched me, like I'm doing it all the time. I don't even know, it's like awesome conscious now. Yeah. Do you do your trial poses to work pleasure and away from pain? Yeah, that's, yeah. I don't consciously think about that, but you can and should for sure. Have you ever asked them a question where they can answer no? No. I don't think no. I'm thinking yes.
